overview: A single lightweight Go-based REST API service exposes a /greeting endpoint returning JSON greetings. The service handles default and personalized greetings, standard error responses, and includes a health endpoint for operational monitoring.
requirements: GET /greeting returns JSON with a message field and HTTP 200
Default greeting 'Hello, World!' when no name provided
Personalized greeting when name query parameter is provided
Sanitize name parameter to prevent injection
Return 405 for unsupported methods, 404 for undefined endpoints, 500 for internal errors
Log all requests with timestamp, endpoint, and status

components.name: greeting-api
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Greeting API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Lightweight REST API providing greeting messages
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:9090
paths:
  /greeting:
    get:
      summary: Get a greeting message
      parameters:
        - name: name
          in: query
          required: false
          schema:
            type: string
          description: Optional name for personalized greeting
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Successful greeting response
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Greeting'
        '405':
          description: Method not allowed
        '500':
          description: Internal server error
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/Error'
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
components:
  schemas:
    Greeting:
      type: object
      required: [message]
      properties:
        message:
          type: string
          example: Hello, World!
    Error:
      type: object
      properties:
        error:
          type: string
components.componentAgentInstructions:
Implement a Go service using net/http listening on port 9090.

Endpoints:
- GET /greeting: If 'name' query param absent or empty, respond with JSON {"message":"Hello, World!"}. If present, sanitize it (strip HTML/control characters, limit length to 100, HTML-escape using html.EscapeString) and return {"message":"Hello, <name>!"}. Respond 200 with Content-Type application/json; charset=utf-8.
- GET /health: return 200 JSON {"status":"ok"}.

Behaviors:
- For /greeting with non-GET methods: return 405 Method Not Allowed with Allow: GET header.
- For unknown paths: return 404 with JSON {"error":"not found"}.
- Wrap handlers with recover middleware returning 500 with JSON {"error":"internal server error"} on panic.
- Logging middleware: log timestamp (RFC3339), method, path, status, duration to stdout.
- Use http.Server with ReadTimeout 5s, WriteTimeout 10s, IdleTimeout 60s to handle concurrency.

Dockerfile: multi-stage build with golang:1.22-alpine builder and distroless or alpine runtime; expose 9090; CMD runs the binary.

Provide go.mod (module greeting-api, go 1.22), main.go, and Dockerfile in /greeting-api.
